audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for plot, Bethany anointing, Judas, Passover meal, covenant blood, Gethsemane, arrest, young man fleeing, trial, Son of Humanity saying, and Peter denial.

verses.restored: Now the Passover and Unleavened Bread were after two days. The chief priests and scribes were seeking how, by deceit, they might seize him and kill him.
For they were saying, Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar of the people.
While he was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, reclining at table, a woman came with an alabaster jar of very costly pure nard ointment. Breaking the jar, she poured it over his head.
Some were indignant among themselves: Why has this ointment been wasted?
For this ointment could have been sold for more than three hundred denarii and given to the poor. And they were scolding her.
But Jesus said, Leave her. Why do you trouble her? She has worked a beautiful/good work in me.
For the poor you always have with you, and whenever you wish you are able to do good for them; but me you do not always have.
What she had, she did. She anointed my body beforehand for burial.
Amen, I say to you: wherever the good message is proclaimed in the whole world, what she did will also be spoken in memory of her.
Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went away to the chief priests so that he might hand him over to them.
Hearing this, they rejoiced and promised to give him silver. And he sought how he might hand him over at a good time.
On the first day of Unleavened Bread, when they sacrificed the Passover, his disciples said to him, Where do you want us to go and prepare for you to eat the Passover?
He sends two of his disciples and says to them, Go into the city, and a person carrying a jar of water will meet you; follow him.
Wherever he enters, say to the house-master, The Teacher says, Where is my guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?
He will show you a large upper room furnished and ready; prepare there for us.
The disciples went out, came into the city, and found it just as he said to them; and they prepared the Passover.
When evening came, he came with the twelve.
As they reclined and ate, Jesus said, Amen, I say to you: one of you will hand me over, one eating with me.
They began to be grieved and to say to him one by one, Surely not I?
He said to them, One of the twelve, the one dipping with me into the bowl.
For the Son of Humanity goes as it has been written about him, but woe to that person through whom the Son of Humanity is handed over; beautiful/good for that person if he had not been born.
As they were eating, taking bread, blessing, he broke it, gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
Taking a cup, giving thanks, he gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, poured out for many.
Amen, I say to you: I will certainly not drink again of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the reign of the Divine.
After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
Jesus said to them, You will all stumble, for it has been written, I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.
But after I am raised, I will go before you to Galilee.
Peter said to him, Even if all stumble, yet not I.
Jesus said to him, Amen, I say to you: today, this night, before a rooster sounds twice, you will deny me three times.
But he spoke emphatically, If I must die with you, I will certainly not deny you. All said likewise.
They came to a place named Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, Sit here while I pray.
He takes Peter, James, and John with him, and began to be deeply distressed and troubled.
He said to them, My soul/life is deeply grieved to the point of death; remain here and keep watch.
Going forward a little, he fell on the ground and prayed that, if possible, the hour might pass from him.
He said, Abba, Father, all things are possible for you; take this cup away from me. Yet not what I will, but what you will.
He comes and finds them sleeping, and says to Peter, Simon, are you sleeping? Were you not strong enough to keep watch one hour?
Keep watch and pray, so that you may not enter testing. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak.
Again he went away and prayed, saying the same word.
Again he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy; and they did not know what to answer him.
He comes the third time and says to them, Sleep the rest and rest? It is enough. The hour has come. Look, the Son of Humanity is handed over into the hands of sinners/misaligned ones.
Rise, let us go. Look, the one handing me over has drawn near.
Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, arrived, and with him a crowd with swords and clubs from the chief priests, scribes, and elders.
The one handing him over had given them a sign, saying, Whomever I kiss, he is the one; seize him and lead him away securely.
Coming, immediately he approached him and said, Rabbi, and kissed him.
They laid hands on him and seized him.
One of those standing by drew a sword, struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
Jesus answered them, Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs to arrest me?
Day by day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not seize me; but let the Scriptures be fulfilled.
Leaving him, all fled.
A certain young man was following him, wearing a linen cloth over his naked body; they seized him.
But he left the linen cloth behind and fled naked.
They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and scribes gathered.
Peter followed him from far away, right into the courtyard of the high priest, and was sitting with the attendants, warming himself at the fire.
The chief priests and the whole council were seeking testimony against Jesus to put him to death, but they were not finding any.
For many were bearing false witness against him, and their testimonies were not equal.
Some stood and bore false witness against him, saying,
We heard him say, I will destroy this temple made with hands, and through three days I will build another not made with hands.
Even so their testimony was not equal.
The high priest stood in the middle and questioned Jesus, saying, Do you answer nothing? What are these testifying against you?
But he was silent and answered nothing. Again the high priest questioned him and said, Are you the Messiah, the Son of the Blessed?
Jesus said, I am; and you will see the Son of Humanity sitting at the right hand of Power and coming with the clouds of heaven.
The high priest tore his garments and said, What further need do we have of witnesses?
You heard the blasphemy. What appears to you? They all condemned him as liable to death.
Some began to spit on him, cover his face, strike him, and say to him, Prophesy. The attendants received him with slaps.
As Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the high priest's servant girls came.
Seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, You also were with the Nazarene, Jesus.
But he denied it, saying, I neither know nor understand what you mean. He went out into the forecourt, and a rooster sounded.
The servant girl, seeing him, began again to say to those standing by, This one is from them.
Again he denied it. After a little while, those standing by again said to Peter, Truly you are from them, for you are Galilean.
But he began to curse and swear, I do not know this person you speak of.
Immediately a rooster sounded a second time. Peter remembered the word Jesus had said to him: Before a rooster sounds twice, you will deny me three times. Breaking down, he wept.
